compiled_state.title: As a developer running /dso:preplanning on a ui_probes:deferred epic, preplanning blocks until brainstorm completes interactively
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Add a "UI Probes Deferred Gate" to `plugins/dso/skills/preplanning/SKILL.md` that detects the `ui_probes:deferred` tag on the epic before proceeding with Phase 1. When the tag is present, halt with a clear message directing the user to re-run `/dso:brainstorm <epic-id>` interactively. When the tag is absent (or the tags field is empty/absent), proceed normally. Mirror the structure of the existing Scrutiny Gate and Interaction Conflict Gate in the same SKILL.md.

**Why**: Without a preplanning-side consumer update, the `ui_probes:deferred` tag (emitted by S5 in non-interactive mode) has no enforcement effect — epics flow through preplanning to implementation-plan with unresolved UX intent, causing the cascading rework this epic aims to prevent. The consumer gate is what makes the deferred tag actionable.

**Scope**:
- IN:
  - New "UI Probes Deferred Gate" section in `plugins/dso/skills/preplanning/SKILL.md`, placed alongside the existing Scrutiny Gate and Interaction Conflict Gate.
  - Presence-based check: halt only when `ui_probes:deferred` is present in the epic's tags array; proceed normally when absent or tags field is empty/absent.
  - Halt message directs the user to re-run `/dso:brainstorm <epic-id>` interactively.
  - Fail-open behavior if `ticket show` fails (treat tag as absent and proceed) — identical to the existing Interaction Conflict Gate.
  - Regression test in `tests/skills/test-preplanning-*.sh` (following existing convention) asserting (a) the gate halts when `ui_probes:deferred` is present, and (b) the gate passes through when the tag is absent.
- OUT:
  - Sprint-side or roadmap-side consumer handling (future follow-on if needed).
  - The tag emission itself (S5).
  - Documentation updates to CLAUDE.md (S7).

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, `plugins/dso/skills/preplanning/SKILL.md` contains a "UI Probes Deferred Gate" that halts the workflow when the epic has the `ui_probes:deferred` tag and directs the user to re-run `/dso:brainstorm <epic-id>` interactively.
  ← Satisfies: epic intent that the `ui_probes:deferred` tag emitted by S5 has real enforcement effect downstream
- When this story is complete, the gate is a presence-based check that proceeds normally when `ui_probes:deferred` is absent or the tags field is empty/absent, and fails-open if `ticket show` fails (identical behavior to the existing Interaction Conflict Gate).
- When this story is complete, a regression test asserts the gate (a) halts when `ui_probes:deferred` is present on the epic and (b) passes through when the tag is absent — consistent with how the Scrutiny Gate and Interaction Conflict Gate are tested.
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic.

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Gate structure and placement should match the existing Scrutiny Gate and Interaction Conflict Gate patterns in `plugins/dso/skills/preplanning/SKILL.md` so the three gates read as one family of tag-based entry guards.
- [Consumer impact] This story closes the enforcement loop for SC7 — SC7 emits the tag, S8 enforces it at the immediate downstream consumer.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
